title: protocol.rs: DirectoryListParams online/offline Vecs unbounded — client OOM from rogue relay
description: DirectoryListParams.online and .offline are Vec<UserInfo> with no length cap. Each UserInfo contains String fields (pub_id, nickname) that are themselves unbounded (tracked by nie-uhyi.4). A rogue or compromised relay can send a directory_list notification with hundreds of thousands of UserInfo entries. The client deserializes the entire Vec before any handler runs, allocating unbounded memory before the application can inspect the size. This is distinct from nie-uhyi.4 (which caps string field length per entry): even with per-field length bounds, a million-entry Vec still causes OOM. Attack path: relay sends directory_list with 1M UserInfo entries, each with a 32-char pub_id and 32-char nickname → ~64 MB per message before any client-side check. Fix: add a MAX_USERS_IN_DIRECTORY constant (e.g. 10_000) and a custom deserializer or post-deserialization length check that returns an error if online.len() + offline.len() > MAX_USERS_IN_DIRECTORY. Affected: all relay-facing clients that deserialize DirectoryListParams (cli, tui, wasm, daemon).
